subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **156 control markers** (26 per player in 6 colors)
- **12 tribe cards** decks (6 used per game): Centaurs, Dwarves, Elves, Giants, Halflings, Merfolk, Minotaurs, Orcs, Skeletons, Trolls, Wingfolk, Wizards
- **1** board (map with 6 territories)
- **Scoring track**
- **Glory tokens** for territories
- **3 dragon cards** (age markers)

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
1. **Choose 6 tribes** (out of 12) to use this game — either random or curated.
2. **Shuffle selected tribe decks** together.
3. **Place board** with territory glory tokens.
4. **Deal 1 card** to each player.
5. **Place remaining cards** as draw deck.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **1 card in hand** to start.
- **Control markers** in your color.
- **Score marker** on 0.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content: Ethnos is a set-collection and area-control game played over 3 ages. Recruit bands of allies (sets of matching tribes or colors) to gain control of territories and score points.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
On your turn, do ONE of:
1. **Recruit** — Draw 1 card (from deck or face-up discard area).
2. **Play a band** — Play a set of cards as a band (same tribe or same color). Activate the leader's ability and place a control marker.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Playing Bands
- A band is a group of cards that share either the same tribe or the same territory color.
- Designate 1 card as the leader.
- **Leader's tribe ability** activates.
- **Leader's color** determines which territory you place a control marker in.
- **Band size** matters: larger bands score more and place stronger control.
- After playing a band, discard all remaining cards in your hand face up.

#### Control
- Place a marker on the territory matching your leader's color.
- At age end, whoever has the most markers in a territory scores its glory token points.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Tribe Abilities (vary by game)
- Each tribe's leader gives a unique bonus when playing a band.
- Examples: Centaurs let you immediately recruit; Wizards give bonus points per wizard in the band.

#### Age End
- When 3 dragon cards are drawn from the deck, the current age ends.
- Score territories and band sizes.
- Shuffle all cards and start the next age.

#### Hand Limit
- Max 10 cards in hand.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### Per Age
- **Territory control** — Most markers in each territory scores glory points (increasing value over ages).
- **Band size bonus** — Bands of 3+ score bonus points (3: 1pt, 4: 3pts, 5: 6pts, 6: 10pts).

#### After 3 Ages
- Highest total score wins.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Discard wisely** — Playing a band discards your whole hand. Time band plays when your hand is mostly used.
- **Territory focus** — Concentrate control markers in 2-3 territories.
- **Big bands** — Band size bonuses are significant; build toward large sets.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Tribe selection** — The 6 tribes chosen at setup drastically change strategy.
- **Dragon timing** — Track dragons drawn; plan last-age actions carefully.
- **Flexible collecting** — Keep options open between tribe-matching and color-matching bands.
- **Deny picks** — Taking face-up discards can deny opponents key cards.
